impact: `src/cache/in-flight.ts:201` computes the parent directory via `final.substring(0, final.lastIndexOf("/"))`. Hard-codes Unix path separators; the rest of the module uses `join()` + `tmpdir()`. On Windows (or any path with a trailing-separator edge case) `lastIndexOf("/")` returns -1, `substring(0, -1)` returns "", the temp file lands in cwd, and rename fails. One-line fix: `import { dirname } from "node:path"` and `const dir = dirname(final)`. Consistent with `cache/session.ts`.
resolution: src/cache/in-flight.ts:atomicWriteFile now uses `path.dirname(final)` instead of `final.substring(0, final.lastIndexOf("/"))`. Handles Windows path separators and trailing-slash edge cases. Matches the cross-platform pattern cache/session.ts uses.
title: atomicWriteFile uses string-split instead of path.dirname; breaks on Windows / edge paths
